# s007 structured commands: placeholder branch creation on take-into-work +
# per-command timeout fail-fast + scalar back-compat.
[!exec:git] skip

exec git init

exec mtt init
cp gated.yaml .mtt/config.yaml

exec mtt add 'implement feature'
stdout 'created t1'

# mtt types surfaces the per-command timeout on the done edge.
exec mtt types
stdout 'timeout 100ms'

# take into work: the tbd -> in_progress gate runs `git checkout -b task/{{.ID}}`.
exec mtt in_progress t1
stdout 't1: tbd → in_progress'

# the branch task/t1 was created. On a fresh repo with no commits the branch is
# UNBORN, so `git branch --list` would be empty — assert the current branch.
exec git symbolic-ref --short HEAD
stdout 'task/t1'

# the in_progress -> done gate is `sleep 1` bounded by a tight 100ms per-command
# timeout under a 5m global: it must fail fast and block (exit non-zero); the task
# stays in_progress.
! exec mtt done t1
stderr 'blocked'
exec mtt show t1
stdout '\[in_progress\]'

# back-compat: a bare-string command edge (tbd -> cancelled: ["true"]) still gates.
exec mtt add 'scrap this'
stdout 'created t2'
exec mtt cancelled t2
stdout 't2: tbd → cancelled'

-- gated.yaml --
version: 1
project:
  name: demo
command_timeout: 5m
types:
  - name: task
    prefix: t
    parents: []
    default: true
    statuses:
      - {name: tbd,         kind: initial}
      - {name: in_progress, kind: active}
      - {name: done,        kind: terminal}
      - {name: cancelled,   kind: terminal}
    transitions:
      - {from: tbd,         to: in_progress, commands: ["git checkout -b task/{{.ID}}"]}
      - {from: tbd,         to: cancelled,   commands: ["true"]}
      - {from: in_progress, to: done,        commands: [{run: "sleep 1", timeout: 100ms}]}
      - {from: in_progress, to: cancelled}
